meta.label: Efo Riro
meta.description: Yoruba-style efo riro: rich spinach stew simmered in pepper-tomato base with palm oil, crayfish, and assorted proteins. Deep, savory, and perfect with swallow or rice.

source.author: Suka Smart Assistant
source.reference: Traditional efo riro profile (pepper base + palm oil + greens) standardized for SSA catalog.

summary: Classic Yoruba efo riro: spinach and/or ugu cooked in a pepper-tomato base with palm oil, crayfish, and optional assorted meats/fish for a rich, savory greens stew.

notes: Naturally gluten-free and dairy-free; confirm bouillon is GF if used.
If using dried fish/stockfish, rinse well and remove bones before serving.
Do not overcook greens; add near the end to preserve color and freshness.

ingredients.label: Spinach, chopped
notes: Classic in many home versions. For more traditional texture, use a mix of spinach + ugu.

ingredients.label: Ugu (fluted pumpkin leaves), chopped (optional)
notes: Optional; if using tougher greens, blanch briefly and squeeze dry.

ingredients.label: Tomatoes (fresh or canned)
notes: If using canned, use crushed or diced.

ingredients.label: Yellow onion
notes: Use half in the blend and half sliced for frying.

ingredients.label: Scotch bonnet/habanero
notes: Adjust to heat preference.

ingredients.label: Red palm oil
notes: Add up to 240 ml for richer, party-style stew.

ingredients.label: Iru (locust beans), optional
notes: Optional; rinse briefly to mellow intensity.

ingredients.label: Salt
notes: Add to taste depending on stock/bouillon.

ingredients.label: Cooked beef (or goat), bite-size pieces
notes: Use leftover cooked beef/goat or simmer fresh until tender first.

ingredients.label: Smoked fish, rinsed and deboned
notes: Optional; adds smoky depth.

ingredients.label: Stockfish, pre-soaked and rinsed
notes: Optional; soak until pliable.

ingredients.label: Stock or water (as needed for consistency)
notes: Use meat stock for best flavor. Add gradually.
A good blender makes a smooth pepper base quickly.

steps.title: Prep greens
steps.body: Wash greens well. If using tough greens (ugu), blanch 1–2 minutes, drain, and squeeze dry. Chop spinach and/or ugu and set aside.

steps.title: Blend pepper-tomato base
steps.body: Blend red bell pepper, tomatoes, half the onion, and scotch bonnet until smooth (or slightly textured if preferred).

steps.title: Cook down the pepper base
steps.body: Heat a pot over medium heat. Add the blended pepper base and cook uncovered, stirring occasionally, until reduced and the raw smell is gone, 12–18 minutes.

steps.title: Fry in palm oil for depth
steps.body: Add palm oil and the remaining sliced onion to the reduced base. Cook 6–10 minutes, stirring, until the oil turns a deeper orange-red and begins to separate slightly at the edges.

steps.title: Season and add proteins
steps.body: Stir in ground crayfish, iru (optional), salt, and bouillon (optional). Add cooked beef/goat and any fish/stockfish. Add a splash of stock/water as needed to keep a stew consistency. Simmer 8–12 minutes to marry flavors and heat proteins through.

steps.title: Add greens and finish
steps.body: Stir in chopped greens in batches. Cook just until wilted and bright, 3–5 minutes. Taste and adjust seasoning and thickness.

steps.title: Serve
steps.body: Serve hot with swallow (eba, pounded yam, amala, fufu) or rice. Cool quickly for storage; refrigerate up to 4 days or freeze up to 3 months.

notes: Signature profile: efo riro is a palm-oil-rich pepper base with greens and crayfish/iru depth.
Oil separation: letting the base cook until the oil starts to separate slightly is key for authentic flavor.
Greens timing: add greens at the end to keep color and prevent bitterness.
Consistency: stew should be spoonable and not watery; reduce longer if needed.

variations.label: Party-rich assorted meats
notes: Increase palm oil to 240 ml and use a mix of beef, goat, shaki/tripe, and stockfish; simmer longer for deeper flavor.

variations.label: Spinach-only quick version
notes: Use only spinach and omit stockfish; keep cook time shorter for a fresh, weeknight stew.

variations.label: Vegetarian efo riro
notes: Omit meats/fish/crayfish. Use mushrooms for umami and add iru/ogiri optionally; season carefully and reduce base well.
